title: Mental Accounting: Why You Blow Your Bonus Every Time
hook: Your brain puts money in invisible buckets — and that one quirk costs you over six thousand dollars a year.
angle: Nobel Prize-winning research shows people spend windfall money 3x faster than regular income — not because they're irresponsible, but because their brain categorizes money by source, breaking the rule that all dollars are equal.
audience: Anyone who has ever spent a bonus, tax refund, or gift card faster than their regular paycheck
key_numbers: 3x faster spending of windfall money vs. regular income
$6,200/year average lost to mental accounting errors
73% of people treat 'fun money' and 'serious money' differently
Only 12% of bonuses are saved vs. 32% of equivalent regular income
